title: my-schedule is the canonical schedule endpoint; others are scope-narrowed views
statement: The user-facing schedule has one canonical endpoint (GET /api/events/my-schedule) that aggregates personal + bookmarked + registered + crew + co-owned events. Other listing endpoints are scope-narrowed views: profile event-registrations is registrations-only, calendar feed is the same scope as my-schedule serialized as ICS, single-event detail uses the same _build_schedule_item_for_event helper to keep its shape consistent.

claims_code.where: events.py:159 — primary aggregator; _build_schedule_item_for_event is the canonical row shape
events.py:637 — single-event detail view; uses _build_schedule_item_for_event to match my-schedule row shape (added 2026-05-09 to drop mySchedule coupling on the frontend)
profile.py:292 — registrations-only narrowed view (subset of my-schedule's coverage)
events.py:277 — ICS-format export with same scope as my-schedule

source: schedule review 2026-05-10 MEDIUM #5; commit 1b5d864 'detail page calls /api/events/{id}/detail, drops mySchedule coupling'
